description_long: This stone block is a roughly hewn, rectangular chunk of granite with a predominantly white and gray coloration, interspersed with black mineral streaks and patches. The surface texture is coarse and uneven, with visible grain and natural fractures, giving it a rugged, unpolished appearance. Some faces show more pronounced striations and color banding, while others are mottled with tan and yellowish hues, suggesting exposure to the elements. The block appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications except for a faint, dark marking on one of the upper surfaces. Its irregular geometry and substantial mass make it suitable as a physical barrier or boundary marker in an urban or landscaped environment. The overall impression is of a durable, natural stone element that could serve both functional and aesthetic purposes in outdoor settings.
description: A rugged, rectangular granite stone block with coarse, mottled white, gray, and black surfaces, featuring natural striations and rough edges. Its solid, heavy appearance suggests use as a barrier or boundary in urban or landscaped areas.
description_view_0: The block's front face is broad and mostly white with vertical black streaks and a rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This side reveals a narrower profile with pronounced vertical striations and a more angular edge.
description_view_2: The rear face displays a mix of tan, gray, and black mottling, with a rougher, more fractured appearance.
description_view_3: This view highlights the top surface with a visible dark marking and a blend of beige and gray tones, as well as chipped corners.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set directly on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Some surface discoloration and chipped corners suggest moderate weathering and exposure to the elements.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: A faint dark marking is visible on the top surface; no engravings or carvings present.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or loosely grouped with other blocks
